channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Landeskog 'excited about what the future looks like' after playing in a game for 1st time in 3 years
item.description: LOVELAND, Colo. (AP) — For the first time in 1,020 or so days, Gabriel Landeskog has some fresh game tape to examine. Game tape of him hitting players, taking hits, being called for a penalty and even putting someone in a headlock.
item.dc:creator: PAT GRAHAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Phil Wertman Invitational: GJ's Bryce Flanagan dominates 800m and more
item.description: The sun scorched down on Stocker Stadium Friday for the Phil Wertman Invitational. But the heat did not stop many of the Western Slope's top athletes from showing off their speed, power and verticals.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: The Masters: Ex-Santa Clara star McCarty’s birdie binge leaves rookie just 3 shots behind
item.description: McCarty's hot streak of 8 birdies in 12-hole span is something his mother has seen since he was at Santa Clara University.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Jokic wraps up triple-double average for season, 3rd NBA player to achieve that feat
item.description: Nikola Jokic will average a triple-double for the season, making the Denver star the third player in NBA history to pull off such a feat.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Rory McIlroy catches fire, shoots 66 to get back into contention at the Masters
item.description: AUGUSTA, Ga. (AP) — Rory McIlroy’s fragile mental state got a big boost when he left Augusta National on Thursday night, shortly after a pair of late double bogeys had cast a pall on an otherwise brilliant round, in time to put his 4-year-old daughter, Poppy, to bed.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Rory McIlroy, Bryson DeChambeau surge; Justin Rose holds slim Masters lead at midway point
item.description: We are in for an amazing finish at Augusta National this weekend after a second round that saw Rory McIlroy and Bryson Dechambeau climb the leaderboard.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Masters: When it starts, how to watch, betting odds for golf's first major of 2025
item.description: AUGUSTA, Ga. (AP) — Justin Rose remained atop a star-studded leaderboard after the second round of the Masters on Friday, with defending champion Scottie Scheffler, Rory McIlroy and Bryson DeChambeau all within three shots of the lead.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bernhard Langer and Fred Couples barely missed the Masters cut. For Langer, it was the final time
item.description: AUGUSTA, Ga. (AP) — More than 30 years ago, Fred Couples helped Bernhard Langer slip back into the green jacket at the Masters. On Friday, both of them nearly made the cut.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Utah scores season-high 7 goals in win over Seattle
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Whether or not they make the NHL playoffs, the Utah Hockey Club is out to finish the season with a bang.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Walter Clayton Jr.'s defensive stop gives Florida its 3rd national title with 65-63 win over Houston
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — Florida’s Walter Clayton Jr. came up with the perfect going-away present for that spirit-crushing Houston defense that bullied, battered and bedeviled him all night. It was a defensive gem of his own. Right before the buzzer. For the win and the national title.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Jaquan Brisker's bond with Bears' DC Dennis Allen predates his NFL career
item.description: Jaquan Brisker was originally drafted by the Saints, but instead joined the Bears, where he will be reunited with his former defensive coordinator, Dennis Allen, who is now the Bears' defensive coordinator.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlin Sharkey, WGN 9

item.title: UConn returns to top of women's basketball, dominates South Carolina 82-59 to capture its 12th title
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (AP) — During the final sweet moments of UConn’s 12th national title, Geno Auriemma and Paige Bueckers embraced after the star guard departed her final game with the Huskies. It was the moment they wanted all along. “They’ve all been gratifying, don’t get me wrong," Auriemma said.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Monarchs suffer first loss of the season in 2-1 battle with Ventura County
item.description: Real Monarchs battled back from a two-goal deficit, pushed late, and nearly snatched a draw from the jaws of defeat for a second straight match - but ultimately fell 2-1.
item.dc:creator: Mattey Casey, Royal Riot Podcast, ABC 4

item.title: Houston, Florida play for title and put a different spin on the underdog role to wrap March Madness
item.description: Coach Kelvin Sampson’s squad of defenders and deniers face Florida for the national title Monday night. They wrap up a front-runner’s Final Four that featured all No. 1 seeds but ends with the two top ones — Auburn and Duke — sitting at home.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: UConn returns to top of women's basketball, dominates South Carolina 82-59 to capture its 12th title
item.description: Dawn Staley and South Carolina hope to finish off a second straight season with an NCAA championship, which would give the Gamecocks a third title in four years. To do so, they'll need to beat Paige Bueckers and UConn on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Strong, Fudd help UConn blow out UCLA 85-51 in Final Four as Bueckers moves 1 win from elusive title
item.description: Sarah Strong and Azzi Fudd didn’t even need much help from superstar Paige Bueckers to propel UConn into the national title game, leading the Huskies to a remarkably easy 85-51 victory over UCLA on Friday night in the Final Four of the women’s NCAA Tournament.
item.dc:creator: Doug Feinberg, Fox 5
